title: TypeScript command generation next-stage review
goal: Identify the next bounded stage of work for generated TypeScript command packages.
scope: TypeScript command-package generation, generated TypeScript outputs, and generated-package proof/conformance checks.
non_goals: Do not implement TypeScript generation changes in this review pass.
Do not promote findings into active planning or create new issues automatically.
Do not review unrelated generated Python relocation work beyond noting that #661 already owns it.

review_mode.review question: What is the next evidence-backed stage for TypeScript command generation after the current root runnable adapter and package metadata fixtures?
review_mode.default finding cap: 2 findings; exceeded to 3 because the package promotion, weak-agent safety, and conformance-harness gaps are distinct next-stage gates.

review_method.commands used: git status --short; rg -n "typescript|commandPackage|generated/typescript|generate.*typescript|node|npm|tsc|vitest|conformance" scripts src generated packages tests .agentic-workspace -S; gh issue list --repo rickardvh/agentic-workspace --state open --limit 20 --json number,title,labels,url; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node
review_method.evidence sources: Static review of command-package IR maturity policy, TypeScript generator, generated root/planning/memory package metadata, generated root CLI handoff, and the generated-package checker; local generated-package static proof and Node conformance passed.

references.label: command package IR
references.role: canonical TypeScript package maturity and command truth

references.label: TypeScript renderer
references.role: generated TypeScript package and CLI output

findings.title: Promote package TypeScript targets from metadata fixtures to runnable read-only adapters
findings.summary: Planning and Memory TypeScript packages are generated only as metadata proof fixtures, while root workspace TypeScript is already a runnable read-only adapter.
findings.evidence: generated/typescript/planning-cli/package.json and generated/typescript/memory-cli/package.json both set fixtureOnly true, omit bin, and use maturity id metadata-proof-fixture; generated/typescript/workspace-cli/package.json sets fixtureOnly false, declares bin, and uses runnable-read-only-adapter.
findings.risk if unchanged: The TypeScript generation lane remains root-only for real invocation, so package-local agent workflows cannot dogfood non-Python read-only adapters and package command generation maturity cannot advance evenly.
findings.suggested action: Create a bounded lane to promote planning-cli and memory-cli TypeScript targets to runnable-read-only-adapter for their read-only generated commands, with package-specific process handoff defaults and local/Docker conformance.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or new GitHub issue for TypeScript package read-only runnable adapters
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when TypeScript generation work resumes or when package-local non-Python adapters are needed for dogfooding.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink to a short reference once package TypeScript targets become runnable and conformance-backed

findings.title: Root TypeScript adapter is runnable but not yet weak-agent safe
findings.summary: The current root TypeScript CLI delegates supported commands to the canonical Python process, but the maturity policy still marks weak-agent routing as review-required rather than allowed.
findings.evidence: generated/typescript/workspace-cli/src/cli.mjs whitelists command names and hands argv to AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_RUNTIME or python -m agentic_workspace.cli; the root TypeScript target maturity is runnable-read-only-adapter with weak_agent_routing review-required.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can technically invoke the generated TypeScript adapter, but the product cannot honestly route weak agents to it as a safe default because help, recovery guidance, and off-happy-path behavior are not yet proven as weak-agent safe.
findings.suggested action: After package runnable promotion or in a separate root-only lane, define and prove the weak-agent-safe-adapter promotion criteria for root TypeScript: help shape, unsupported-command recovery, runtime failure mapping, and proof output that explicitly advertises the routing status.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or new GitHub issue for weak-agent-safe TypeScript root adapter promotion
findings.promotion trigger: Promote after read-only conformance is stable enough that weak-agent routing would reduce operating cost without reducing safety.
findings.post-remediation note shape: delete after weak-agent-safe status is encoded in IR and checked by generated-package proof

findings.title: Conformance coverage is manually mirrored instead of derived from command conformance refs
findings.summary: The generated-package conformance checker hardcodes selected command cases and output-field projections instead of deriving coverage from command_package_ir conformance_refs.
findings.evidence: scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py defines many _selected_* helpers and compare_adapter calls for individual commands, while command_package_ir.json already lists conformance_refs for generated commands.
findings.risk if unchanged: Each promoted TypeScript command or package target requires manual checker edits, which increases drift risk and makes the next generation stage more expensive than the IR contract implies.
findings.suggested action: Create a contract-driven conformance harness that enumerates generated commands and selected process fixtures from conformance_refs, leaving only command-specific selected-field extractors where truly necessary.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or new GitHub issue for contract-derived generated TypeScript conformance
findings.promotion trigger: Promote before broadening runnable TypeScript packages beyond the current root command set.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink after the checker consumes conformance refs directly

recommendation.promote: Promote the next executable TypeScript generation stage as package TypeScript runnable read-only adapters, with conformance-harness derivation either included as the first milestone or split as a prerequisite if scope needs to stay smaller.
recommendation.defer: Defer weak-agent-safe root routing until runnable read-only conformance is less manual and package targets are no longer metadata-only.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not use #661 for this work; it is specifically about relocating generated Python adapters after landing, not advancing TypeScript maturity.

retention.trigger: After the next TypeScript generation lane is promoted or explicitly declined.
retention.proof surface: Generated-package IR maturity fields, generated/typescript package metadata, scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py, and generated-package conformance output.
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

drift_log: 2026-05-07: Review created from maintainer request to identify the next TypeScript code-generation stage.
